title: Listen and Choose a Response — Housing & Dining

questions.stem: The dining hall has been serving the same meals every week. I'm getting tired of it.

options.A: You should fill out the feedback form. They actually consider student suggestions.
options.B: Dining halls serve hundreds of students every day.
options.C: I usually cook my own meals in the dorm kitchen.

questions.explanation: The speaker is complaining about repetitive food, so suggesting a constructive way to address the issue is helpful.

questions.stem: Can I have a guest stay overnight in my dorm room over the weekend?

options.A: Overnight guests are allowed but need to be registered at the front desk.
options.B: Dorm rooms are quite small for two people.
options.C: I had a friend visit last weekend.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about dormitory rules, so explaining the policy clearly is the most helpful response.

questions.stem: I'm thinking of switching from the 14-meal plan to the unlimited plan. Can I do that?

options.A: Yes, you can change your meal plan during the first two weeks of the semester.
options.B: Unlimited meal plans are more expensive.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about changing a service plan, so confirming it's possible and explaining the window for changes is helpful.

questions.stem: The refrigerator in my dorm room stopped working. All my food is spoiling.

options.A: That's urgent. I'll send a maintenance worker to your room right away.
options.B: Refrigerators can be repaired or replaced.
options.C: You should keep a cooler for emergencies.

questions.explanation: The speaker is reporting an urgent maintenance issue, so taking immediate action to resolve it is the appropriate staff response.

questions.stem: We should decorate our dorm room together. It looks a bit plain right now.

options.A: Good idea! I can bring some posters and string lights from home.
options.B: Dorm rooms come with basic furniture.
options.C: I'm not very good at decorating.

questions.explanation: The speaker is suggesting a shared activity, so agreeing enthusiastically and offering to contribute is cooperative.

questions.stem: When do I get my housing deposit back after moving out of the dorm?

options.A: The deposit is refunded within 30 days after move-out, minus any damages.
options.B: Housing deposits are required for all residents.
options.C: I got my full deposit back last year.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about a refund timeline, so providing the specific timeframe and relevant conditions is direct and helpful.

questions.stem: Does the dining hall offer gluten-free options? I have celiac disease.

options.A: Yes, we have a dedicated gluten-free station. Just ask the staff for assistance.
options.B: Gluten-free diets have become more common.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about a dietary accommodation, so confirming availability and providing specific guidance is the appropriate response.

questions.stem: Your room looks so cozy! I love how you arranged the furniture.

options.A: Thank you! I spent the whole weekend rearranging everything.
options.B: Dorm rooms are usually small and compact.

questions.explanation: The speaker is giving a compliment about the room, so graciously accepting and sharing a bit about the effort is the natural social response.

I'm thinking of switching from the 14-meal plan to the unlimited plan. Can I do that?
